title: Lymph node follow-up

referral.text: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.
referral.rationale: Relevant to persistent or unexplained neck-lump recognition with recommendation qualifiers, not diagnosis.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.5 — laryngeal, oral, and thyroid cancer recognition features

follow_up.text: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.
follow_up.rationale: Relevant only to recommendation-qualified lymphadenopathy with specified respiratory, age or smoking context; it does not support generic node follow-up.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.6 — lung and pleural cancer respiratory features

follow_up.text: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.
follow_up.rationale: Relevant to clinician-documented review and safety net when symptoms remain unexplained; it does not assign an interval or action automatically.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

additions.text: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.
additions.rationale: Relevant only to recommendation-qualified lymphadenopathy with specified respiratory, age or smoking context; it does not support generic node follow-up.
additions.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.6 — lung and pleural cancer respiratory features

It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Node site, size, duration, tenderness, mobility, progression, systemic symptoms, infection context, age and examination are absent.
No malignancy, infection, test, imaging, biopsy, referral or urgency is inferred.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
